rationale: In the conceptual framework, Choi and Fishbach (2011) is cited in the core theory-building section for H2 rather than as a generic background reference. The citing paper uses it as evidence that internal versus external motivations can switch across sequential activities after perceived progress. That logic is then carried into the shopping-trip context to predict changing unplanned purchase motivations by impulsivity level. Even though it appears in a grouped citation, its functional role in hypothesis justification is clear.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Choi and Fishbach (2011)'s motivation-switching insight to a new context: unplanned purchases during a shopping trip. It uses that prior result to motivate predictions that consumers balance internal and external motivations over sequential in-store decisions.
